entry.title: T CODE SHOWING MATERIAL AN HS CODE
entry.content: I am looking for a TCODE that if i input multiple material codes would show me the HS code also. I am aware of MM03 however is there any others?

entry.title: Re: Exploring Integration Options in SAP Datasphere with the focus on using SAP extractors
entry.content:
Fantastic post @ClaudiaFiess !

Do you know if there is a similar post for non-SAP sources?

As per documentation, to achieve data federation with non-sap sources, the only connectors currently supported are generic OData and JDBC. Is this still the case?

Any available options (to achieve federation) for when source systems only provide traditional REST API's (non-OData) ?

Thanks!

Silvio Canha

 

SAP Datasphere

entry.title: Re: ABAP RUNTIME ERROR WHEN RUNNING THE ME2J report
entry.content:
Hi @Nava123 

As your report times out you need to either narrow down the selection criteria so the report retrieves less data and runs faster or run the report as a background job.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: SAP Content - Wrong short Text in Table T247 for Month March Entry DE language
entry.content:
Hello everyone
I have a question about the content of table T247. The month of March is abbreviated incorrectly in German, or how do you see it? Is this already wrong in the SAP standard/content? The abbreviation MRZ should be used here instead of MAR, right?

entry.title: Re: Change column in Adaption Project
entry.content: Hi Thomas - are you able to describe more in details what you exactly did to achieve and "override" of the column using the columnKey? I'm doing an adaption project as well, where I need to redefine a columns content, so I create a column fragment, and a cell fragment. But when I define the p13n data with the correct columnKey as the one I want to replace, it's tells me that there is duplicate columnKeys.

entry.title: CBO UI App - Custom Catalog Extensions - Add to Business Catalog
entry.content:
Hi,

In SAP Public Cloud we have several CBO with UI applications.According to document documentation Custom Catalog Extensions are generated, and after we need to assign it to a Business Catalog.(only standard one - in the popup to select business catalogs).

How can we add to Customer Business Catalog(YY*)? I need to create separare business roles to restrict access to CBO UI Apps and with standard ones it's not possible.

Adding the customer root certificate in the Keystore would be enough to make the connection, it should be enough right?

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/basic-authentication-concept-outbound

Source location of item 000010 must be a shipping point w/o deviating address

Message no. /SCMTMS/LOG_INT084

Any idea on how to fix this problem? Thanks a lot!

entry.title: SAP Build App Web APP deployment Error
entry.content:
Hi,

I got the folowing Error, when I try to build my Application as a Web APP. 
I use the only Version that is available for me at the moment: 4.9.218

Runtime version: 4.9.218; AB version: 2.1.14COMMAND:
/usr/local/lib/node_modules/@sapappgyver/orchestra-builder-npm/node_modules/rnv/bin/index.jsconfigure -p web -s standalone -c standalone --ci --yes --skipRnvCheck --packageManager yarn --template @sapappgyver/orchestra-template-standalone@4.9.218 --hooks --npxMode
FAILED with ERROR:
Command failed with exit code 1: /usr/local/lib/node_modules/@sapappgyver/orchestra-builder-npm/node_modules/rnv/bin/index.js configure -p web -s standalone -c standalone --ci --yes --skipRnvCheck --packageManager yarn --template @sapappgyver/orchestra-template-standalone@4.9.218 --hooks --npxMode

 How can I fix this issue?

Best Regards,
Manuel

entry.title: Re: Can you remember the first time you heard of SAP
entry.content:
Hi @HARIKRISHNAM - the reason you cannot yet write a blog post is because you are a new member of SAP Community since Wednesday. There is a wait period before a new member can write a blog post, and once submitted the blog post goes into a queue to be handled by moderators. Please also ensure that you have completed all steps within the Tour the SAP Community 2024 and not just ticked through to completion.

Thanks for joining our community! We look forward to reading your blog posts in the near future and hope you get as much back in return - which I'm sure you will!

entry.title: Re: Understanding the Price Category information
entry.content:
Dear @Tayane ,

Thanks for the blog.

With regards to  "Own/customer created applications and Business Catalogs have no pricing and therefore the have no influence on the FUE licensing." 
Is this something true for S4HANA Cloud 2SL landscape?
Basically we have custom applications developed via BTP and they are deployed on S4C(2SL) using custom Catalog extension on Business Catalog.
Would they be considered as part of license(because even though they are custom but they reside on std. Business Catalog)
If yes , then how can these custom applications be moved out to something "no pricing catalogue" because it is not possible to create custom catalog in 2SL it seems.

Thanks,
Anoop

entry.title: Re: CBC 2SL customer using CLT service
entry.content:
Configuration Localization Tool (CLT) cannot be enabled/activated for SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2-System Landscape systems. currently CLT is not supported for 2SL customers. 

Please note that CBC-CLT is only available for 3SL systems/customers.

CLT is still in the early adopter phase for 3SL customers, it can be enabled if following prerequisites are met:

  • Partners and Approved customers.
  • Partners and customers which have a 3SL landscape and only if demo data is not enabled and deployed for the project.

This below SAP KBA informs you about the current limitations, restrictions, and additional information for using CLT:

3107866 - SAP Central Business Configuration : Configuration Localization Tool restrictions and limitations.

Thanks,

Guru Yog

entry.title: Re: blind scan
entry.content:
For information, for the fun, I could do the code from scratch in two hours by applying Sudoku solving algorithms - Backtracking (brute-force search) - Wikipedia), no need to convert C code.

"A brute force algorithm visits the empty cells in some order, filling in digits sequentially, or backtracking when the number is found to be not valid. Briefly, a program would solve a puzzle by placing the digit "1" in the first cell and checking if it is allowed to be there. If there are no violations (checking row, column, and box constraints) then the algorithm advances to the next cell and places a "1" in that cell. When checking for violations, if it is discovered that the "1" is not allowed, the value is advanced to "2". If a cell is discovered where none of the 9 digits is allowed, then the algorithm leaves that cell blank and moves back to the previous cell. The value in that cell is then incremented by one. This is repeated until the allowed value in the last (81st) cell is discovered."

To help you start and learn by yourself, here are the start and the end of the program. I didn't use any subroutine/method, just loops and conditions. It must be approximately 120 lines of code.

TYPES tv_row TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF i WITH EMPTY KEY.
TYPES tt_cell TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF tv_row WITH EMPTY KEY.

DATA(cells) = VALUE tt_cell(
    ( VALUE #( ( 5 ) ( 3 ) (   ) (   ) ( 7 ) (   ) (   ) (   ) (   ) ) )
    ( VALUE #( ( 6 ) (   ) (   ) ( 1 ) ( 9 ) ( 5 ) (   ) (   ) (   ) ) )
    ( VALUE #( (   ) ( 9 ) ( 8 ) (   ) (   ) (   ) (   ) ( 6 ) (   ) ) )
    ( VALUE #( ( 8 ) (   ) (   ) (   ) ( 6 ) (   ) (   ) (   ) ( 3 ) ) )
    ( VALUE #( ( 4 ) (   ) (   ) ( 8 ) (   ) ( 3 ) (   ) (   ) ( 1 ) ) )
    ( VALUE #( ( 7 ) (   ) (   ) (   ) ( 2 ) (   ) (   ) (   ) ( 6 ) ) )
    ( VALUE #( (   ) ( 6 ) (   ) (   ) (   ) (   ) ( 2 ) ( 8 ) (   ) ) )
    ( VALUE #( (   ) (   ) (   ) ( 4 ) ( 1 ) ( 9 ) (   ) (   ) ( 5 ) ) )
    ( VALUE #( (   ) (   ) (   ) (   ) ( 8 ) (   ) (   ) ( 7 ) ( 9 ) ) ) ).

DATA(backup_cells) = cells.

DATA(row_match) = 0.

DATA(row) = 1.
DATA(col) = 1.
DO.
  " 0 means that the digit is to be determined
  IF backup_cells[ row ][ col ] = 0.

    ...

  ENDIF.

  IF backup_cells[ row ][ col ] <> 0
      OR digit_determination_succeeded = abap_true.
    " go to the next cell
    IF col < 9.
      col = col + 1.
    ELSEIF row = 9.
      EXIT. " solved !
    ELSE.
      row = row + 1.
      col = 1.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.
ENDDO.

ASSERT cells = VALUE tt_cell(
    ( VALUE #( ( 5 ) ( 3 ) ( 4 ) ( 6 ) ( 7 ) ( 8 ) ( 9 ) ( 1 ) ( 2 ) ) )
    ( VALUE #( ( 6 ) ( 7 ) ( 2 ) ( 1 ) ( 9 ) ( 5 ) ( 3 ) ( 4 ) ( 8 ) ) )
    ( VALUE #( ( 1 ) ( 9 ) ( 8 ) ( 3 ) ( 4 ) ( 2 ) ( 5 ) ( 6 ) ( 7 ) ) )
    ( VALUE #( ( 8 ) ( 5 ) ( 9 ) ( 7 ) ( 6 ) ( 1 ) ( 4 ) ( 2 ) ( 3 ) ) )
    ( VALUE #( ( 4 ) ( 2 ) ( 6 ) ( 8 ) ( 5 ) ( 3 ) ( 7 ) ( 9 ) ( 1 ) ) )
    ( VALUE #( ( 7 ) ( 1 ) ( 3 ) ( 9 ) ( 2 ) ( 4 ) ( 8 ) ( 5 ) ( 6 ) ) )
    ( VALUE #( ( 9 ) ( 6 ) ( 1 ) ( 5 ) ( 3 ) ( 7 ) ( 2 ) ( 8 ) ( 4 ) ) )
    ( VALUE #( ( 2 ) ( 8 ) ( 7 ) ( 4 ) ( 1 ) ( 9 ) ( 6 ) ( 3 ) ( 5 ) ) )
    ( VALUE #( ( 3 ) ( 4 ) ( 5 ) ( 2 ) ( 8 ) ( 6 ) ( 1 ) ( 7 ) ( 9 ) ) ) ).

entry.title: SAP CloudSupport Alerts
entry.content:
I am getting SAP CloudSupport alerts by e-mail from <sapcloudsupport@alerts.ondemand.com> on planned and unplanned maintenance breaks of SAP CPQ Production system.

But there is no possibility to subscribe to similar alerts on non-productive environments.

How could I (or a colleague) start receiving those e-mails, too?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP CloudSupport Alerts
entry.content: Please checkout this KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/2900069 on how to subscribe for notifications as per your tenant.

entry.title: Re: Export PDF via API Gateway for Unicode (Khmer Fonts)...

Hi Channa,

SAP HANA or MS SQL version?

Do you have all the fonts which are required?

If HANA, please apply the solution from SAP KBA 3367780.

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Business One Support

entry.title: SAP Certification Transformation: How You Can Get and Stay Certified
entry.content:
It’s no secret: Technology is changing faster than most businesses can manage. Well-trained talent is increasingly scarce, and digital skills gaps keep getting wider. To keep up with the ongoing changes in talent and technology, both learners and organizations need to be agile and adaptive to keep pace with the competition.   

At SAP, our mission is to create opportunity through learning and development for all. And with the need for fast and comprehensive upskilling, we are transforming our SAP Certification program, making continuous learning a priority. That way, you can always prove you’re up-to-date with the latest innovations. 

Here’s what this means for you: 

Get certified with trusted certifications, tailored to your role. 

Transforming our certification approach means making things easier for you. Demonstrate intermediate or advanced skills with out of over 80 globally recognized role- and product-based certifications across key SAP solution innovation areas. Focusing on the core skills and competencies, our role-based certifications, like this one for SAP S/4HANA Cloud implementation consultants, make your skillset more valuable as it gains sustainability and more relevance for longer periods of time.   
Good news: the enhanced SAP Learning Hub includes four certification exam attempts per subscriber per year and all learning resources to prepare for the certification exams. 
 

Stay certified and keep your skills up to date with the latest innovations. 

As a second step in our certification transformation, we are focusing on opportunities for you to keep your skills sharp. As of this April, certified learners need to continuously keep their skills up-to-date and certifications valid with evolving products by completing mandatory assessments before their certification expires. With an SAP Learning Hub subscription, you can prepare for and take the stay certified assessments at any time during the validity period of your certification. 

The standard validity of certifications is 12 months, with an initial validity end date of March 31, 2025. Every time you complete an assessment to stay certified, the validity period extends by 12 months. Easy enough, right? 

For sure! So, this is how you can keep your certifications valid: 

  1. Log into the ‘My Learning’ page with your SAP Universal ID (Note: your SAP Universal ID must be linked with a secondary email account from another email domain provider) 
  2. With a click on “Certifications” section you will find your passed certification(s) with the respective validity date.  
  3. Access the enablement materials that are linked to prepare for the stay certified assessment. You need an SAP Learning Hub subscription to access stay certified assessment. 
  4. Take the stay certified assessment linked to your certification. Once successfully passed, the new validity date of your certification will be updated. 

If you are already certified in an SAP solution, you will receive a personalized email informing you about the required next steps to ensure that you don’t miss your certification expiry date. So don’t worry, we’ve got you covered! 

We truly believe that the redesigned SAP Certification program will make it easier and faster for you to get and stay certified, to identify next career steps and goals, and ensure you have the latest skills and valid credentials on the newest SAP innovations tailored to your role and responsibility. With a subscription to the enhanced SAP Learning Hub, you can have access to all the learning resources you need to get and stay certified in one place. So why don’t you set yourself up for success? 

For more information, please visit the SAP Certification transformation page.  

Please share any feedback or questions you have! 

#SAP Certification #SAP Learning Hub

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK application login with ADFS in internal network
entry.content:
Hi,

by enabling Certificate-Based Authentication suggested by @bill_froelich and @Jitendra_Kansal the login popup show correctly and user can login in the app. Great!!

But we can't execute the logout. If I click the logout the app show the initial screen. Then proceed but the app skip the login page and connect with the previous user. I've tried to disinstall and reinstall the app and also to delete registration in "User registration" tab on Mobile cockpit but the app login with the same user.

Thank you.

Regards.

entry.title: Re: "Bitmap file: No color table available (True Color, Bitcount 32)"
entry.content:
Hello there,
I used the following steps:
1) edit the .bmp image using paint
2) save as bmp picture in paint specifying as 16bit instead of 24bit

Only drawback is it reduces the color quality greatly, but it is successfully uploaded in sap se78

With regards,
Sourav Gupta

entry.title: Re: Change datasource of Analysis for Office workbooks
entry.content:
The solution that @TammyPowlas  provides here works.

Hoever you must keep in mind that ALL of your Design Rules will be Deleted! So make a BKP of the WB before changing the Source.

In case you have lots of cstom formats and rules ... youll have to manually transfer them from the backup WB.

Regards,
Boyan.

Vedette

entry.title: Re: BAPI_BUPA_ADDRESS_CHANGE does NOT update email address.
entry.content:
Put 'X' in FM - BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT .

Like this. -  

 CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
    EXPORTING
      wait = 'X'. 

This will solve your error.

entry.title: SIC Code in Plant level
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community
I need to find the table/field of SIC code in a plant level. 
Similar to SIC Code for Item level which can be found in MVKE-MVGR1

Thank you

entry.title: Re: How to track exchange rates for outgoing payment in d...
entry.content: We have resolved the issue by creating manual Journal double entries for the Payment (can mimic Journal preview from a unposted Outgoing payment), when you do this, we can post to bank GL in USD. Then use BP Internal Rec to close and match the AP Invoice against Journal to close the AP.

The problem is:

1. I cant see the PIRs in the app Manage Buffer Levels

2. The PIRs are not affecting the ADU calculation

Hopefully someone has a solution 🙂

KR

entry.title: Re: System log in SLT
entry.content: Hello Andrey,, Thank you so much for clarifying..

entry.title: Re: SAC catch error from Planning Sequence
entry.content:
Dear @ERivetti,

I'm using the following code to stop the execution of my function:

 

var DataActionResponse = DataAction.execute();
if (DataActionResponse.status !== DataActionExecutionResponseStatus.Success){
break;
}

 

I hope this helps

Best regards
Susanne

entry.title: Re: Electronic Invoicing in Malaysia
entry.content:
Hello everyone , I just want to add regarding the MY E-Invoicing workshop.

We've already conducted the MY E-Invoicing workshop - 2 series of the SAP E-Invoicing workshop on Dec 2023 and February 2024. Recordings and materials are now available upon request. for SAP Customers only [Registration | MY SAP e-Invoicing Workshop].

Please stay tune and check this site under events for the next workshop / scheduled date:

https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/document-reporting-compliance

Thank you

entry.content:

Please find the below Screen shots.

entry.title: Same Mapping Different WSDL
entry.content:
Dear Integration fellows,

I have developed an extension of IDoc and will upload it into CPI, so that the IDOC is getting recognized when sending over CPI. However, when replacing the old wsdl with the new in Graphical Mapping in CPI, all the mappings I have done are marked as deprecated and cannot be used. 

This means I have to do all the mapping from begining. Is there a way to avoid this and copy the mapping to the new wsdl structure?

entry.title: Convert Journal Voucher to Journal Entry via DI-API
entry.content: Is it possible to convert a Journal Voucher to Journal Entry via DI-API? I can only see the Add method for Journal Vouchers and nothing else. Just wondering if there is another object or service we should be using?

entry.title: The use of "Bank" role in BP and Bank Hierarchy in S/4 HANA Cloud
entry.content:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/186460fdc35a4b64a713da9bb00deb1e/a1758116d9b24d48a99e3fa61af5de89.html?locale=en-US&version=2402.501

Kind Regards,

Eitaro

entry.title: Re: Generation repository objects ADT
entry.content:
Hi,

I am not sure why you are unable to see the "Error Message" details in the UI, seems like a bug. Have you updated your ADT to the latest one? If not, please do. 

The error message should say this  Add "local last changed" admin field ( ABP_LOCINST_LASTCHANGE_TSTMPL,ABP_LOCINST_LASTCHANGE_UTCL ) and "last changed" admin fields (ABP_LASTCHANGE_TSTMPL, ABP_LASTCHANGE_UTCL ) to the reference table.

Hope this helps.

Thanks and Regards,

Shyam

entry.title: Re: Is it possible we put a Water Mark on the data we exp...
entry.content:
Yes, it's possible to add a watermark to data exported from SAP Cloud for Customer (C4C) Data Workbench. A watermark is typically added to documents or exported data to indicate the source, status, or other relevant information. Here's how you might achieve this:

1. **Pre-processing in C4C Data Workbench**: Depending on the capabilities of C4C Data Workbench, you may be able to add a custom field or attribute to your data records that serves as a watermark. This could be a field indicating the export date, export user, or any other relevant information you want to watermark the data with. Ensure that this field is populated correctly before exporting the data.

2. **Export Data with Watermark Field**: When exporting data from C4C Data Workbench, include the watermark field in your export configuration. This ensures that the watermark information is included in the exported data file.

3. **Post-processing in External Tools**: After exporting the data from C4C Data Workbench, you can post-process the exported file using external tools or scripts to add a visual watermark. For example, if you're exporting data to a CSV or Excel file, you can use scripting languages like Python or PowerShell to modify the file and add a watermark column based on the watermark field values.

4. **Automate the Process (Optional)**: If you need to add watermarks to exported data regularly, consider automating the process using scripting or scheduling tools. This ensures consistency and reduces manual effort.

5. **Review and Validate**: Before distributing or using the exported data, review it to ensure that the watermark information is correctly applied and meets your requirements. Validate the watermark against the original source data in C4C to ensure accuracy.

By following these steps, you can effectively add a watermark to data exported from SAP Cloud for Customer Data Workbench, providing additional context or information to the exported data. Be sure to consider any regulatory or compliance requirements when adding watermarks to sensitive data.

entry.title: Re: Mark Date field as Nullable in .cds file for CAPM pro...
entry.content: Dear @RalfHandl,

entry.title: Re: Mark Date field as Nullable in .cds file for CAPM pro...

entry.title: Re: Flexible Workflow using Responsibility Management in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hello @chelliah_soundar ,

Thanks for the blog - very good summary & guide!

I have tried setting this up and... failed.

The according Teams and Responsiblity "function" is - according to SAP Help only available for item-based approval of PRs. See:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/af9ef57f504840d2b81be8667206d485/91ab576eaf58475f8934d2dcef16edb3.html?locale=en-US&version=2021.001

I have set up the same thing in my system and am able to see the function for

  • item-based PR approval
  • PO approval
  • Contract approval

but NOT for the overall release of the PR.

How did you do this? Did you do some magic / additional customizing that I cannot see?

Looking forward to your response!

KR
Hermann

entry.title: SKU CODE STATUS (ACTIVE/ OBSOLETE)
entry.content:
Hi

In FMCG what is the Difference or meaning of the various status

DChain-spec status

X-distr.chain status

Plant-sp.matl status

X-plant matl status

I need to know which of this columns would give me the accurate active or obsolete status of a SKU code. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: No product is displayed in "My Purchased Products" in...
entry.content:
Hello,

If you follow the "Get Assistance" guidance for Finance & Legal questions in SAP for Me (lower left in navigation bar), it points you for contract related questions to contact the SAP Order Management team via email: sapcontractcenter@sap.com

There is a KBA Note: https://me.sap.com/notes/0001660069

Issue might be a delay in processing. Otherwise, best for investigating technical issues is to submit a case (incident).

Best regards.

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK application login with ADFS in internal network
entry.content:
You can enable EnableOverrides property to true in the ConnectionSettings block in BrandedSettings.json.

This will allow user to either proceed with current connection settings or scan a new on-boarding QR code.

entry.title: Alert for an critical authorization assignment to the user
entry.content:
Our requirement is to perform a realtime monitoring for the critical profiles assignment for the dialog and non dialog users.To generate an alert when critical profiles are assigned.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP for Me Portal Latency Issues Again
entry.content:
Hello Julie,

I would like to follow up with you on this topic. Please could you contact me with more details via email: **REMOVED BY MODERATION** 

Thanks and best regards,

Ozzy

entry.title: Re: Can we create complex transactional FIORI apps using...
entry.content: If you have a complicated validation logic and you want to encapsulate this logic in a ABAP model class, this class is not persistent over serveral roundtrips due to the REST API conecpt. Thus, the validation has to be made on client side, which is very bad, since all the information for validation is on server side. E.g. the concept of restful applications in the context of complicted business transactions with user interaction is a stupid idea.

entry.title: Re: Mark Date field as Nullable in .cds file for CAPM pro...
entry.content: Well, "OnStockDate":"" is as wrong as "OnStockDate":"Z" would be. The UI must send "OnStockDate":null if the date should be null.

entry.title: Re: SAP CRYSTAL REPORT 2020 support ODBC DRIVER 18 FOR SQ...
entry.content:
I believe this post not Answered yet,

On further investigation, we installed the latest crystal report version 2020 and tried “Set Location” and “Verify Database” using the ODBC datasource “ODBC Driver 18 for SQL Server” unable to perform both above activities, even with a new SP created and linked.

However, these activities were working when we use the Datasource configured with “ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server” .

Hence, It would be helpful, if you could get some technical assistance on launching the Crystal report that uses the stored procedure via the “ODBC Driver 18 for SQL Server”, would help our investigation.

Do SAP Crystal Report have support for SQLProcedures function when Datasource is used with “ODBC Driver 18 for SQL Server”?

- Suresh Balasubramanian

entry.title: Re: Alert for an critical authorization assignment to the...
entry.content:
Through System Monitoring there is a metric called Number of users with critical profiles  under SAP ABAP 7.10 and higher template

Metric description: This is the number of users with critical profiles. By default, all users with profile SAP_ALL are listed here. 

The output of corresponding metric will return text like:

Reference:0ALERT;VIRTUAL; # Items (NonCompliant/Compliant/NotFound/NotValuated/All): XXX/   0/   0/   0/ XXX
To run Configuration Validation use the URL please: <URL>

This metric is not checking particular user, it will review all system users with profile SAP_ALL assigned.

Any other metric behavior will require a custom development, review:

2484872 - Requested metric not included in standard templates

Development of Custom RFC data collector guide

entry.title: Re: April (Citizen) Developer Challenge – SAP Build Apps: Task 1 - S/4HANA OData
entry.content:
I'd just have one question : I wasn't able to fulfill this task until I set the TrustAll property in the Destination Configuration to True. I previously had the "Use default JDK truststore" checkbox set and I wasn't able to fetch the correct data. 

Any ideas on what could I do to be able to complete the exercise without having TrustAll in my destination config?

entry.title: Re: Mark Date field as Nullable in .cds file for CAPM pro...
entry.content: Dear @RalfHandl, Since on UI since I am not entering any value in the date input field, so it is expected that it will take the blank value, and in the payload I can see the blank value. However framework throws error with blank value. How and Why will the UI pass null ?

entry.title: Re: Authenticating Users in The Power Platform (Entra ID)...
entry.content:
This will not work - since you do not only require the certificate but also the corresponding private key (which should never be disclosed).

You seem to be seeking for a Principal Propagation mechanism (in a system-to-system communication).
Maybe OAuth 2.0 (with Authorization Code Grant and PKCE) would be more appropriate.

entry.title: Re: Open SAP GUI in separate window with AIE
entry.content: @JaySchwendemann Probably with the scripting ocx: sapfewse.ocx. But this is only a conjecture.

entry.title: Re: Mark Date field as Nullable in .cds file for CAPM pro...
entry.content: How to make the UI pass null depends on the UI techonology you are using. Please add this information to your question.

entry.title: Re: Mark Date field as Nullable in .cds file for CAPM pro...
entry.content: No, what I mean is, why is it expecting me pass a null value? Can't I simply keep the Date field as blank ?

entry.title: S/4HANA Public Cloud: Read and access restrictions to specific G/L accounts
entry.content:
Hello!

We are wondering how we could give access and read restrictions to heterogeneous set of G/L accounts and hide rest of the COA like Authorization group function in ECC. Though the similar field seems to be available in S4HC, we cannot find possibility to restrict the role regarding it.

We have tried to make these restrictions by Functional area restriction, but it has zero effect to account visibility. Also we tried G/L account hierarchy restriction (HRYTYPE_HRYID_GL), which also did not help.

Is there any other possibilities how we could proceed with this issue?

BR,

Anssi Murtonen

entry.title: Re: Mark Date field as Nullable in .cds file for CAPM pro...
entry.content: What I believe is in Odata we have an option to add a nullable property to our Date fields, if we want to keep it blank, however I see that option is not available here. Is it possible to mark Date field as Nullable so that it accepts blank values as well ?

entry.title: Re: Can we create complex transactional FIORI apps using...
entry.content: so what should be the approach to develop fiori apps for such scenarios? should we remove client side validations, take all the input from the user and send it to backend through multiple odata services. And process the input data in backend and display the result back to client ?

entry.title: Re: MDG-F Hierarchy Assignment with Derivation
entry.content:
Hello,

We have same issue, can anybody help us about hierarchy derivation?

entry.title: Re: Practical introduction to SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine
entry.content:
Thank you for this helpful article. What is the function to_real_value doing with the float array? I.E. how is this vector stored on DB side?  -

In my understanding the interesting point is to find a suitable vector space model for real world data, e.g. tokens from a large corpus, as e.g. word2vec. Storing a vector in a DB is maybe useful but not the main problem.

entry.title: Re: EC Business rule for custom field picklist
entry.content:
Hi Elizabeta,

If you would like to filter on countries then you can link your picklist to a parent picklist. For that you would need to have a custom country field in Comp as well. You can find here more infos:

2861689 - How to create MDF Picklist and set a parent-child relationship between MDF picklists? - SAP for Me

2476080 - Employee Central: Child Picklist not filtered by Country (Parent) - SAP for Me

Furthermore, you can create a custom mdf instead of a picklist and work with associations. I think this is actually the better option, since you can reference your mdf object to a field in Job Info (so country or legal entity does not need to be in Comp). The best option would be to associate your mdf object to Legal entity and enter the following field criteria in the details of your custom field (see screenshot).

Best,

Susan

entry.title: Re: RISE WITH SAP S/4 HANA PUBLIC CLOUD Manage and Classification of Commodity code for a Product.
entry.content:
Hi @former_member818101 , @Babu_Lal_Limba , 

Do you know if exist the possibility to upload the list of the commodity codes for intrastat process (S/4hana public cloud)? Our customer has more than 30K products and need a mass load to register all the stadistical codes. 

Can you help me? 

Or do you know if exist an API to create a custom App (UI5) to create all the commodity codes with a template? I have searched in SAP API Business Hub, and I didn't found. 

Thanks a lot! Regards

#intrastat #s4hanapubliccloud #commoditycode

entry.title: Disconnecting archiving with sendout of documents from SAP
entry.content:
Hello,

we are currently facing a serious problem with our archiving solution, which is also causing failures to send out any emails with invoices from SAP. This is becoming a critical issue for us and we are looking for an alternate temporary solution to the problem.

Is there a possibility to disconnect/unlink the archiving with sendout of docs from SAP? We would like to be able to send out invoices from SAP without archiving them (until the archiving system is available).

Thank you in advance for your advice.

entry.title: Re: March Developer Challenge - CloudEvents: Week 4
entry.content:
Thank you @ajmaradiaga for quick answer and starting services and thank you for great challenge.
Everything worked fine.

entry.title: SAP GENAI Deployment URL is not able to connect in POSTMAN
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

SAP GENAI deployment URL is not able to respond the query back. I followed the same tutorial:Prompt LLMs in the generative AI hub in SAP AI Core & Launchpad | SAP Tutorials

any suggestion please?

Thanks,

Padmindra

#genai #LLM #SAPGENAI

entry.title: Connection issue between SAP MII and SAP ECC
entry.content:
Calling FM through SAP MII BLS transaction and getting desired output when executed BLS transaction in MII workbench.

If i called BLS transaction through URL getting "Name or Password incorrect" error.

Checked credentials and connections setting looks good which is used in SAP JCO call.

entry.title: Re: Activity Types and Service Materials for Project Based Services and Project Billing
entry.content:
Hello,

We have the following situation: our customer has intercompany services - assigned in PC as they are not ressource hours based. We are still in the starter system with SAP standard masterdata. 

What I did: I copied activity type U006 to U006Z. I created a material U006Z (copied from U006). Planned the activity cost rates, and the external prices (PSP0). I set up the connection of activity type to material (103637 Maintain Billing Profile). Everything works (I can post the activity, I can see it in both company codes, I see the costs and the planned revenue on the project and I can bill the customer).

What does not work is the Intercompany Billing. For the Intercompany Billing it seems that something is missing - it does not find the material for the pricing. I tried a lot of variances already, but instead of taking the price from my newly created U006Z material, it always pulls T999 miscalenous services and gives a pricing error. But: the miscalenous services have always the amount of the PC I posted.

Any idea what could be missing?

Best regards
Anja

entry.title: Re: March Developer Challenge - CloudEvents: Week 4

Hello,

I'm a little late but better late than never.

entry.title: Re: March Developer Challenge - CloudEvents: Week 4
entry.content: Glad to know that you enjoyed the challenge.

entry.title: KANBAN issue straight to cost center after status FULL
entry.content:
Dear Gurus,

I am looking for an advice what is required in order to achieve when KANBAN card is set to FULL it will automatically issue material to COST CENTER.

The current scenario:

Supply area X is assign to storage location AAA which is bulk storage location via PK05

Control cycle created with replenishment strategy Y which is tied with warehouse storage location BBB.

Two KANBAN status maintained as a full or empty.

Storage type is tied with storage location AAA with stock transfer 311

For replenishment strategy we use as below:

When the card is set to empty it will issue the stock from bulk location AAA to cost center, then TR/TO is created with replenishment stock to replaced within the same location, next time the card is set to empty it will again issue the stock on bulk location and stock transfer another quantity.

The goal is to change it, when status is set to full it would automatically issue stock to cost center without any stock transfer. I have tried to change under replenishment strategies "Procurement for cost center" instead of "Post withdrawal" but without a success.

entry.title: Disconnecting archiving with sendout of documents from SAP
entry.content:
Hello,

we are currently facing a serious problem with our archiving solution, which is also causing failures to send out any emails with invoices from SAP. This is becoming a critical issue for us and we are looking for an alternate temporary solution to the problem.

Is there a possibility to disconnect/unlink the archiving with sendout of docs from SAP? We would like to be able to send out invoices from SAP without archiving them (until the archiving system is available).

Thank you in advance for your advice.

entry.title: Re: The requests received by the Report Processing Server have the format of an old version
entry.content:
This post shows CR4R is becoming EOL, So not sure if making the report in a lower version then updating CR and then re saving would be an issue or not?

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/moving-from-crystal-reports-for-enterprise-to-crystal-reports/ba-p/13579905

entry.title: Re: Practical introduction to SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine
entry.content:
Thanks. I don't think SAP released info on the internal representation of these vectors. My best guess at the moment is they store it as some sort of special blob because if you just SELECT the table you get a binary representation of them (but you can do to_nvarchar() on them and get back the float values as text).

It is true that for a RAG scenario HANA Cloud DB is only giving you the ability to efficiently store, retrieve and query vector for similarity. The conversion of tokens/document chunks into embeddings needs to be done elsewhere.

entry.title: Re: Can you remember the first time you heard of SAP
entry.content:
Hello Katherine!

  We have had offline chats about blog content moderation, and I wanted to share here about the 3 types of reviewers an author might encounter: moderators, owners, and curators. All content is subject to moderation; some topic spaces may have additional rules deemed by owners and/or curators. Did I get that right?

entry.title: Talent Intelligence Hub - Myths and Misconceptions - Part 2 (Migration)
entry.content:
Talent Intelligence Hub (TIH) enables customers to capture the knowledge, abilities, and experience of their workforce and leverage that information to fuel all their talent management processes.

While working with customers and partners, I came across numerous questions, myths, and misconceptions. This blog is the second of a three-part series that aims to highlight the most common misunderstandings that I encountered and provide the necessary insights to debunk those myths.

Part 2 focusses on the myths about the migration to Talent Intelligence Hub. There appears to be some uncertainty about what exactly happens when you hit the button to start the migration.

If you missed the first part of this series, called Talent Intelligence Hub - Myths and Misconceptions - Part 1 (Prerequisites), you can find it here.

 

Myth #1 – Job Profile Builder (JPB) goes away when you turn on TIH

This is probably the most common myth I have heard of so far. I would like to address this misconception thoroughly in order to debunk it once and for all. If you are using JPB and you migrate to Talent Intelligence Hub, JPB remains your job architecture framework and will not be eliminated.

I believe there may be some confusion because there are currently three different situations a customer can be in regarding their competency and job architecture framework, as illustrated below.

 

Let’s take a quick look at these three scenarios:

1. Job Description Manager (JDM)

The oldest framework of all currently existing frameworks is used to manage both the job architecture (legacy job families and job roles) and legacy competency libraries.

2. Center of Capabilities (CoC) and Job Profile Builder (JPB)

If you migrated to JPB after the introduction of CoC or deliberately turned on CoC, you are managing your job architecture and legacy skills through JPB and your competencies through Center of Capabilities.

3. Talent Intelligence Hub (TIH) and Job Profile Builder (JPB)

This is the ideal state for a customer’s system, where competencies, skills and other attribute types are managed through TIH while the job architecture remains in JPB.

 

In principle, there is a fourth possibility, which is managing your job architecture on JDM and migrating to TIH for managing competencies and skills. While this is technically possible, we highly recommend that all customers move away from the old frameworks and manage their job architecture in JPB and their competencies and skills in TIH.

 

Myth #2 - You can transfer your legacy skills to TIH

As explained above, customers who are on Job Profile Builder and Center of Capabilities have access to the legacy skills feature. If you are actively using this feature, meaning you have skill data in JPB, you can’t migrate legacy skills to TIH, regardless of whether you are working with this data or not.

Currently, we cannot migrate legacy skills from JPB to TIH. Therefore, any customers using legacy skills need to take the following steps:

  • Remove all connections between the legacy skills and job roles, job profiles, employees etc.
  • Once all connections have been removed, delete the legacy skills from JPB

If you wish to continue working with these skills and have a history of skill ratings, you have two options. You can either wait for the migration to become possible or create backups of the skill data and skill ratings. After the successful migration, you can import these backups to TIH.

 

Myth #3 - TIH is only available on certain data centers

This misconception is likely caused by the numerous recent AI innovations, some of which are currently restricted to certain data centers, such as Joule.

Fortunately, I have good news for you! Talent Intelligence Hub is accessible in all data centers and can be activated through the Upgrade Center.

 

Myth #4 - Enabling TIH only requires a single click of a button

The honest response is: It depends! 😉

In principle, it is easy to migrate to Talent Intelligence Hub. As mentioned above, all you need to do is navigate to the Upgrade Center, select Talent Intelligence Hub and click the button that will trigger the migration.

However, it is highly likely that there is some data cleanup you’ll need to perform first or a setting that needs to be enabled before you can switch on TIH. This is why the Check Tool is your best friend!

If you’ve never worked with the Check Tool before, simply think of it as a built-in tool in SuccessFactors for performing system health checks that can uncover issues related to data and configuration and provide guidance on how to fix them. A detailed guide can be found here.

Before you start the migration, I highly recommend performing all checks first and resolving any issues that the Check Tool finds. These checks are:

1. In the “System Health” tab, run the checks for Center of Capabilities, Job Description Manager and Job Profile Builder

2. In the “Migration” tab, run the checks for Center of Capabilities, Job Description Manager and Talent Intelligence Hub

Most check results can be resolved by customers without any external help. In a few cases, however, you might need to reach out to your implementation partner or SAP.

Only once all the Check Tool results are positive can you successfully migrate to TIH.

 

My next blog will be the last part of this three-part series and cover myths and misconceptions related to Talent Intelligence hub features and functionality.

Is there anything else you’re not sure about regarding the migration to TIH? Please share your questions via the comments section.

entry.title: Re: The SAP Cloud Platform SDK for Android is GA!
entry.content: Exciting news! The SAP Cloud Platform SDK for Android has officially reached General Availability (GA)! This powerful tool empowers developers to build robust Android applications seamlessly integrated with SAP Cloud Platform services. With features like offline data synchronization, push notifications, and secure authentication, developers can create scalable and enterprise-grade apps with ease. While this SDK caters to Android developers, SAP also offers solutions for iOS Application Developers , ensuring a cohesive experience across platforms. Embrace the future of mobile app development with SAP Cloud Platform SDK and unlock endless possibilities for innovation and efficiency in your enterprise applications.

entry.title: How to maintain number range by selecting object in S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
SNRO option is not available in the Public Cloud.  How can we maintain number range by selecting Object Name: MATERIALNR?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to maintain number range by selecting object in S...
entry.content: In S/4HANA Cloud, T-CODE SNRO is unavailable, please use SSCUI 102282 "Define Number Ranges for Each Material Type" to maintain material number range.

entry.title: Navigating the Data-Driven World of Procurement Analytics: The Roadmap to Achieving Best-In-Class St
entry.content:
In the rapidly progressive digital era, procurement analytics mastery has emerged as a crucial strategic asset, providing crucial insights for enhancing business efficiency and fortifying performance. But how does one truly excel in this integral domain? This guide outlines a comprehensive pathway to achieving elite status in procurement analytics.

Data Quality and Governance: The Cornerstone of Analytical Mastery

The first step to mastering procurement analytics involves effective data management. Access to accurate, complete, consistent, and timely data is vital for insightful analysis. A study by The Hackett Group cites that 70% of businesses find their data quality impeding their ability to capitalise on digital platforms. The implementation of concrete policies, standards, and protocols for data collection, validation, integration, storage, and protection significantly enhances your analytical prowess.

Blend and Integrate Data Sources

Judicious procurement decision-making demands access and fusion of data from a plethora of internal and external sources. These may encompass comprehensive data comprising procurement, finance, sales, inventory, customer, supplier, ERP, market, and industry specifics. Research in the Journal of Theoretical and Applied Electronic Commerce has revealed that productive data unification results in annual savings of 3% for businesses. Employing tools such as data warehouses, data lakes, APIs, and ETL processes can optimise this integration process.

Harness the Potential of Data Analysis and Visualisation

Extracting insightful data, the crux of procurement analytics, using analytical techniques like descriptive, diagnostic, predictive, prescriptive analytics and graphical representations such as dashboards, reports, charts and maps, aid in drawing these insights. Gartner reveals that businesses utilising visual data discovery are apt to find timely information 28% more than others.

Nurturing a Data-Driven Culture along with Capability Enhancement

A successful data analytics strategy encompasses more than just sophisticated tools; it requires a shift to a data-driven corporate culture. This shift has been ratified by a NewVantage Partners survey, with 92.2% of businesses acknowledging people and processes as the major roadblocks to adopting Big Data and AI in daily operations. Emphasise on honing the skills of procurement staff and partners while encouraging data usage and analytics in day-to-day tasks and strategic planning can promote a culture emphasising curiosity, collaboration, learning, and innovation.

With the roadmap clearly laid out above, remember that the journey to mastering procurement analytics is dependent on quality data, wholesome data integration, solid data analysis and visuals, and an unwaveringly data-centric organisational culture. Keeping these aspects in focus will enable your procurement analysis efforts to push your company to new heights amidst stringent market competition.

In conclusion, resource investment in honing your procurement analytics capabilities will not only help you keep pace with rapid advancements, but also firmly position you at the vanguard of best-in-class performers. Don't miss the opportunity to bolster your procurement strategy with data analytics today and embrace the challenges of the future with confidence.

Feel free to read my previous blog on Boosting Business Success with Procurement Analytics: A Journey Paved with Numbers

SAP Ariba Spend Analysis

entry.title: Re: Can you remember the first time you heard of SAP
entry.content:
Hello Jim,

yes, but the owners and curators relate to SAP Community Groups only, not the general SAP Community blog boards - so the onus is on those roles in those "spaces" to review and approve content for their group. Helps to get the right content in the right space!

entry.title: Re: Error message
entry.content:
Hi Tonio,

I would recommend to direct your inquiry to our SAP IT to accurately identify the problem and provide better recommendation for the solution. As I understand if you are using an SAP provided laptop then SAP Logon App is available in Software Center App. But if it is different then your local IT should be able to help.

I hope this helps!
Paulo M.

entry.title: Re: 'INTO' is invalid (due to grammar) error while SQL in BAPI
entry.content: What is the error that is returned?
